hypothesis: If a 3D electron-density encoder is trained with a contrastive objective against a rotation-invariant SMILES encoder, then its embeddings will become pose-invariant—yielding higher unseen-rotation retrieval accuracy and better property-structure clustering and linear-probe prediction than unimodal 3D or SE(3)-equivariant baselines—because the contrastive alignment forces different poses of the same molecule to map to the same SMILES representation.
